_comment:
Committed fixture: a real ItemResponse.model_dump(mode='json') payload from agent_gtd. Regenerate from an agent_gtd checkout via: python -c 'from agent_gtd.models import ItemResponse; from datetime import datetime, UTC; import json; r = ItemResponse(id="11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111", project_id="22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222", title="Register the talos-* engine family", description="Grounded 2026-07-09.

## Intent
Register five talos engines...", status="active", priority="normal", due_date=None, completed_at=None, created_by="cli", assigned_to="", waiting_on="", sort_order=0.0, labels=["engine"], version=3, locked_by_rollout_id=None, build_engine="talos-haiku", acceptance_criteria=["ENGINE ROSTER: registers 5 engines", "ENV OVERLAY: pure function", "EXIT-CODE mapper: 0/10/20/1 distinct"], files_to_modify=[{"path":"src/agent_gtd_dispatch/talos.py","change":"NEW module: TaskSpec + env overlay + argv + result mapping."},{"path":"src/agent_gtd_dispatch/engines.py","change":"Register 5 talos Engine instances + TALOS_ENGINES + is_engine_available gating."}], scope_out=["Do NOT touch frontend/**"], created_at=datetime.now(UTC), updated_at=datetime.now(UTC), blockers=[]); print(json.dumps(r.model_dump(mode="json"), indent=2))'. The keys downstream consumes (talos.serialize_task_spec) are title, description, acceptance_criteria, and files_to_modify — the rest is preserved so a fixture-drift test on the producer side catches a rename.
title: Register the talos-* engine family
description:
Grounded 2026-07-09.

## Intent
Register five talos engines...

files_to_modify.change: NEW module: TaskSpec + env overlay + argv + result mapping.
Register 5 talos Engine instances + TALOS_ENGINES + is_engine_available gating.
